title: senecio ochoanus
intro: Senecio ochoanus, also known as Ochoa's groundsel, is a plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Mexico. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Senecio ochoanus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a useful remedy for skin conditions and infections. Herbal preparations of Senecio ochoanus can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
